title: Align Python signed-frame interface contract
description: Post-commit review of b4ebb84af found Python interface protocols and a LinkLayer helper still describe or implement Schnorr-48 as payload-appended data. The migrated wire format stores the 48-byte signature in MIC. Remove stale payload stripping/contract semantics and add focused regression coverage so public consumers receive the complete authenticated payload.
acceptance_criteria: Python interface contracts state signatures reside in MIC; no receive path strips 48 payload bytes for signatures; focused tests prove signed payloads are delivered intact; Python link tests and three independent reviews pass.
notes: Completed in 8e62f5cbc. Updated Python public contracts and frame docs for Schnorr-48-in-MIC semantics; made RxFrame satisfy exported RxResult via read-only forwarding properties; added independent receive assertions for intact payload/MIC and flattened metadata. Validation: 306 link/vector/interop tests passed with 11 skips; focused 96 passed with 8 skips; Ruff and targeted mypy clean; three final current-marker reviews found no issues.
close_reason: Completed in 8e62f5cbc; Python public and runtime frame contracts now match MIC signatures
